learning_outcomes.description: Articulate the foundational knowledge in the area of entrepreneurship.
Develop, evaluate and communicate actionable business opportunities as part of a startup team.
Create viable and innovative Business Models.
Critically analyse knowledge creation in the startup process.
Practice real-time entrepreneurial leadership to craft ideas and test impacts.

assessments.due_date:
Pitch 20/03/2026

Video 15/05/2026 2:00 pm

Each presentation earns up to 20 marks against the A1 rubric
assessments.task_description:
The presentation arc requires a facilitated pitch delivered in real-time to your peers (deliver in week 4), and a video articulating your venture growth strategy (upload in week 11) . Each presentation format evidences your critical thinking for your team venture and attract up to 20 marks each, against the A1 rubric.

Within each team, the impact of each team member will be appraised using the Buddycheck platform integrated to Blackboard, generating an individual peer assessment factor (PAF). The assigned team score for each presentation x your individual PAF will yield your individual mark for each of the pitch, video & autopsy.

This assessment will evaluate students’ Influential Communicator skills for UQ Business School Assurance of Learning (AoL) program, which maintains accreditation with AACSB and collects student grades in relevant criteria for quality measurement of student learning outcomes across the School. This program ensures that students attain the UQ Graduate Attributes, through alignment with UQ Business School program learning outcomes, and the School continuously improves curriculum. 

AI Statement

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Each presentation format is uploaded via Blackboard (to allow marking allocations & peer appraisal via Buddycheck)
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Case topic 1 27/03/2026 - 2/04/2026

Case topic 2 17/04/2026 - 24/04/2026

Case topic 3 1/05/2026 - 8/05/2026

Case topic 4 22/05/2026 - 29/05/2026

Each student generates ONE resource and moderates THREE resources of other students, for each case topic, within each available date range. Each case topic attracts up to 5 marks.
assessments.task_description:
For each of the 4 case topics, generate a resource using the aligned entrepreneurial theory. Your peers will moderate your topic exploration shown in the resource you produce: for each case topic, each student will create 1 resource & moderate 3 others (up to 5 marks per case topic).

These case topics serve as a safe learning environment for students to test their thinking and gain moderated feedback from the class, using the A2 rubric. By moderating others, each student gains deeper understanding of the respective framework as they learn from the cohort. Each of the 4 case topics will be released in Blackboard with resource creation & moderation completed in RiPPLE. Each topic attracts up to 2 marks for resource creation and 3 marks for moderation (up to 1 mark per moderation submitted)

AI Statement

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Use the RiPPLE link provided in Blackboard>Assessment for each case topic
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Details for a live case will be releaed prior to the designated exam time, allowing you to research the case with the resources provided and to prepare your notes for the exam, to demonstrate your analytical thinking. One double-sided page of handwritten or types notes is permitted to be taken into the exam and must be submitted along with your completed exam paper.

The exam will evaluate students’ Courageous Thinkers skills for UQ Business School Assurance of Learning (AoL) program, which maintains accreditation with AACSB and collects student grades in relevant criteria for quality measurement of student learning outcomes across the School. This program ensures that students attain the UQ Graduate Attributes, through alignment with UQ Business School program learning outcomes, and the School continuously improves curriculum. 

AI Statement

This exam is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit completed paper to the invigilator at the end of the exam session, including your written/typed note sheet.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: The exam paper with your notes must be handed to the invigilator at the close of the exam session.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
S1 | New Ventures

Introduce entrepreneurship, a history of entrepreneurial thought and its place in today's economy. Opportunity discovery & creation. Projects to develop. Assessment timelines. Student team formation.
- Difference between small firms, entrepreneurial ventures and larger
corporations
- Entrepreneurial teams

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: S1 | New Ventures Introduce entrepreneurship, a history of entrepreneurial thought and its place in today's economy. Opportunity discovery & creation. Projects to develop. Assessment timelines. Student team formation. - Difference between small firms, entrepreneurial ventures and larger corporations - Entrepreneurial teams

learning_activities.topic:
S2 | Lean business models

Using lean startup methods to shape business models and expose underlying growth drivers of value. Building a business model canvas informed using lean methods


Learning outcomes: L01, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: S2 | Lean business models Using lean startup methods to shape business models and expose underlying growth drivers of value. Building a business model canvas informed using lean methods

learning_activities.topic:
S3 | Discovery-driven Planning

Developing assumptions to bring new products & services to market. Crafting an idea canvas. Exploring a DDP case.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: S3 | Discovery-driven Planning Developing assumptions to bring new products & services to market. Crafting an idea canvas. Exploring a DDP case.

learning_activities.topic:
S4 | Present venture pitch

Deliver class presentation in your teams, showing how business model enables growth


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S4 | Present venture pitch Deliver class presentation in your teams, showing how business model enables growth

learning_activities.topic:
S5 | Entrepreneurial Strategy

Entrepreneurial strategy frameworks: forming opportunity to gain competitive advantage & growth. Framing observed alignments to the four value-creation dynamics across customer-technology-capabilities-competition elements.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: S5 | Entrepreneurial Strategy Entrepreneurial strategy frameworks: forming opportunity to gain competitive advantage & growth. Framing observed alignments to the four value-creation dynamics across customer-technology-capabilities-competition elements.

learning_activities.topic:
S6 | Public holiday

Use this time and the following mid-semester break to prepare for the next team presentation

Learning outcomes: L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S6 | Public holiday Use this time and the following mid-semester break to prepare for the next team presentation

learning_activities.topic:
S7 | Business Building

Applying a discovery-driven growth orientation to scale with technology & business models
learning_activities.topic_description: S7 | Business Building Applying a discovery-driven growth orientation to scale with technology & business models

learning_activities.topic:
S8 | Entrepreneurial Analyses

Tools to interpret key attributes of proposed or existing firms, to leverage underlying value and to frame strategic capability-growth actions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: S8 | Entrepreneurial Analyses Tools to interpret key attributes of proposed or existing firms, to leverage underlying value and to frame strategic capability-growth actions.

learning_activities.topic:
S9 | Disruptive innovation

How technological & business model innovations can exploit asymmetric forces, for startups to displace resource-rich incumbents, or yield incremental improvements in product or service deliveries. Examine how innovations disrupt incumbents by crafting new market dynamics for 'good enough' products & services that substitute or complement current offerings

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: S9 | Disruptive innovation How technological & business model innovations can exploit asymmetric forces, for startups to displace resource-rich incumbents, or yield incremental improvements in product or service deliveries. Examine how innovations disrupt incumbents by crafting new market dynamics for 'good enough' products & services that substitute or complement current offerings

learning_activities.topic:
S10 | Entrepreneurial Hypergrowth

Exploring hypergrowth using chasm theory as a mechanism to predict technological dispersion so to pre-empt destructive growth forces.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S10 | Entrepreneurial Hypergrowth Exploring hypergrowth using chasm theory as a mechanism to predict technological dispersion so to pre-empt destructive growth forces.

learning_activities.topic:
S11 | Emergent Leadership

Drivers for design-led growth strategies and how to organise for emergent leadership paths in entrepreneurial ventures. Organising new venture using 2 different frameworks with distinct philosophies

Learning outcomes: L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S11 | Emergent Leadership Drivers for design-led growth strategies and how to organise for emergent leadership paths in entrepreneurial ventures. Organising new venture using 2 different frameworks with distinct philosophies

learning_activities.topic:
S12 | Entrepreneurial Finance

Exploring NPV and reverse financials as tools to interpret growth assumptions to accommodate uncertainty in finance models. Apply finance models to prospective ventures to interpret capital needs & long-term value potential including returns to founders & investors

Learning outcomes: L01, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: S12 | Entrepreneurial Finance Exploring NPV and reverse financials as tools to interpret growth assumptions to accommodate uncertainty in finance models. Apply finance models to prospective ventures to interpret capital needs & long-term value potential including returns to founders & investors

learning_activities.topic:
S13 | Navigating Uncertainty

Review key components of entrepreneurship theory & practice, pursuing opportunities beyond resources to hand. Reflecting on the journey through the course, discuss the team autopsy & preparing for the final case study assignment released & due during examination period.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S13 | Navigating Uncertainty Review key components of entrepreneurship theory & practice, pursuing opportunities beyond resources to hand. Reflecting on the journey through the course, discuss the team autopsy & preparing for the final case study assignment released & due during examination period.
